deck.title: Signal
deck.tagline: Swiss-modern rhythm with cobalt interruption slides and orange utility accents.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.subtitle: A modernist system with stark contrast, electric color blocks, and disciplined grid rules
introduction.summary: This sample reflects the second reference image: black/white foundations, cobalt stage panels, orange highlight accents, and clean diagrammatic pacing.

cards.title: Hard Contrast
cards.body: Keep surfaces mostly white or black and reserve cobalt for interruption slides and callout blocks.

cards.title: Utility Orange
cards.body: Orange should mark emphasis, data thresholds, and directional hints — never the whole composition.

cards.title: Grid Discipline
cards.body: Columns, labels, and captions should align precisely; visual looseness breaks the style immediately.

cards.title: Systemic Tone
cards.body: The deck sounds analytical and declarative rather than lyrical or narrative-heavy.

talkingPoints: Use one strong interruption color per spread.
Treat headings as labels inside a system, not decorative typography.
Favor thin rules and modular blocks over shadows and glow.
Let diagrams and data marks feel engineered.
introduction.callout: The deck works because the system is stricter than the content.

color-system.title: Color
color-system.subtitle: Primary palette notes for the Swiss-modern reference

cards.title: Cobalt
cards.body: Use for interruption slides, key labels, and big stage-setting moments.

cards.title: Orange
cards.body: Use sparingly for warnings, active data points, and directional emphasis.

cards.title: Black
cards.body: Ground information-dense spreads with black panels when you want maximum authority.

color-system.callout: If every slide is loud, none of them are. Save cobalt for impact.

type-rules.title: Type Rules
type-rules.subtitle: Before/after guidance for keeping the visual language crisp

cards.title: Headlines
cards.challenge: Loose editorial copy and soft hierarchy weaken the system feel.
cards.fix: Use compact, declarative headings with clear rank and strong margins.

cards.title: Captions
cards.challenge: Floating annotations become visual clutter when they ignore the grid.
cards.fix: Align every caption, kicker, and note to the modular column structure.

cards.title: Data Labels
cards.challenge: Decorative colors can make charts feel playful instead of precise.
cards.fix: Use black, gray, cobalt, and one accent orange with disciplined repetition.

cards.title: Rhythm
cards.fix: Alternate quiet white slides with high-contrast interruption spreads.

type-rules.callout: This style is less about flair and more about compositional law.

system-flow.title: System Flow
system-flow.subtitle: A compact process sample for charts, maps, and diagram-driven pages
system-flow.callout: Charts should feel like parts of the operating system, not pasted-in widgets.

data-story.title: Data Story
data-story.subtitle: Sample narrative blocks for turning metrics into a gallery-style systems deck

cards.title: Frame
cards.body: Start with one declarative black slide that names the thesis plainly.

cards.title: Interrupt
cards.body: Use a cobalt spread to mark the section break or introduction moment.

cards.title: Evidence
cards.body: Follow with white slides carrying tables, charts, or type specimens.

cards.title: Resolve
cards.body: Close with an orange-accent sentence or summary line that feels earned.

data-story.callout: Think sequence first: black, cobalt, white, white, accent.
